item.title: What Comes After AI Translation in Sports Broadcasting?
item.description: The next evolution of fan engagement is personalized commentary. Lingopal CEO Casey Schneider explores what comes after real-time AI translation at IBM's Sports Tech Founder Showcase during NY Tech Week.

item.title: AI Sports Jargon Translation Accuracy 2026
item.description: Explore the accuracy of AI translation for technical sports broadcast jargon in 2026. Discover Lingopal's insights and improve your understanding. Learn more!

item.title: 10 Facts News Media Leaders Can't Ignore About AI Translation in 2026
item.description: The future of news isn't just faster. It's multilingual, accessible, and global by default.

item.title: The Future of Sports Fan Experience Is Multilingual
item.description: This week at SBJ Tech Week, one of the most interesting conversations happening across the sports industry wasn’t only about streaming quality, AI analytics, or immersive stadium technology.

item.title: Global Accessibility Awareness Day (GAAD): Why Multilingual AI Communication Is Becoming Essential for Digital Accessibility
item.description: Every year, Global Accessibility Awareness Day reminds organizations of a growing reality: Digital accessibility is no longer just a compliance requirement. It is becoming a core business, communication, and inclusion strategy.

item.title: AI Dubbing & Live Translation: The New Language Layer
item.description: The internet is global. Speech is not. More than 6.12 billion people were online at the start of April 2026, equal to 73.8% of the global population.

item.title: AI Translation vs. Traditional Methods: Why Live News Needs Real-Time Multilingual Processing
item.description: Live news has always been a race against time. A storm changes direction. A minister resigns. A court ruling lands. A goal is scored in extra time. A breaking story unfolds on camera before the newsroom has time to package it.

item.title: Breaking Language Barriers in Education: From Access to Understanding with Real-Time AI Translation
item.description: Education is only effective when it's understood. Yet for hundreds of millions of learners worldwide, language remains the first and most persistent barrier.

item.title: The 2026 World Cup: The Biggest Audience in History - and the Untapped Opportunity for Global Sports Media
item.description: The next FIFA World Cup 2026 isn't just another tournament. It's shaping up to be the largest, most globally consumed sporting event ever created.

item.title: Live Streaming Accessibility in 2026: From Compliance to Competitive Advantage
item.description: Accessibility in live streaming has moved far beyond its original role as a compliance requirement. What was once treated as an add-on, captions here, translation there, is now becoming a core part of how content is distributed, experienced, and scaled globally.

item.title: LINGOPAL Wins 2026 NAB Show Product of the Year Award
item.description: LAS VEGAS — Lingopal announced last Wednesday (April 22nd - 2026) that its Real-Time Live Translation is an Intelligent Category winner in the 2026 NAB Show Product of the Year Awards. This official awards program recognizes some of the most significant and promising new products and technologies showcased by exhibitors at NAB Show.

item.title: Welcome to Lingopal
item.description: A quick look at how Lingopal is moving its website and editorial workflow into Astro.

item.title: AI dubbing for broadcasters: what teams need now
item.description: Modern dubbing workflows need to protect tone, speed, and operational simplicity, not just raw translation quality.

item.title: How AI translation changes newsroom workflows
item.description: News teams can publish faster and reach multilingual audiences without rebuilding their editorial process from scratch.

item.title: Measuring ROI for live translation
item.description: The return on multilingual live coverage comes from audience growth, retention, and faster content reuse after the event.
